message:
fix(reactor): harden io_uring token handling and add 32-bit platform support

Three related safety improvements to the reactor token subsystem:

io_uring.rs:
- Add token_to_user_data() that validates tokens fit in u64 and rejects
  values colliding with WAKE_USER_DATA / REMOVE_USER_DATA sentinels,
  preventing silent misrouting of completion events
- Add user_data_to_token() that filters out internal sentinel values when
  converting CQE user_data back to tokens, replacing the previous unsafe
  direct cast (Token::new(user_data as usize))
- Wire both functions into register(), submit_poll_remove(), and the
  completion event loop
- Add tests: reserved-value rejection, sentinel filtering, and
  registration-count leak prevention

token.rs (SlabToken):
- Add cfg(target_pointer_width) branches for to_usize() / from_usize()
  so 32-bit platforms pack generation into upper 8 bits and index into
  lower 24 bits instead of assuming 64-bit usize layout
- Add MAX_GENERATION constant (u32::MAX on 64-bit, 0xFF on 32-bit)
- Mask generation wrapping in remove() and retain() to stay within
  platform-specific bounds

uring.rs:
- Update UringReactor doc comments from "Phase 0 stub" to "legacy
  compatibility shim", directing callers to IoUringReactor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
